rationale: Beyond a passing background mention, the cited paper is explicitly used as a methodological precedent in the citing paper. The authors state that their political identity measure based on media habits (Fox vs. CNN/MSNBC) in Studies 4 and 5 is based on Han et al. (2019), and they reference Han et al. again when discussing comparable operationalizations. The citation also appears in their literature framing of political identity effects, but the measurement reuse makes its role clearly substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Han et al. (2019) as a template for operationalizing political identity via media-habit indicators (and related ideological classification logic) in its own studies. It uses the cited work to support implementation of this measure in a new domain, supplemental education preferences.
